marketState: neutral, fundamentals and technicals in conflict
summary: Seasonality data (vs-median, probability-higher, reliability) is entirely null in this snapshot, so the top-priority input for the long-term view is unavailable and conviction is capped. Weather demand is running above 5-year normal in both the 1-7D (+4.3%) and 8-16D (+15.4%) windows, a near-term bullish support for burn, while front price ($2.885) sits almost exactly at fair value ($2.923, -$0.038 spread, FAIR status) with the fair-value curve drifting higher through +21D ($3.021) into the seasonal winter build. Storage is a clear bearish counterweight at +180 Bcf above the 5-year average with a bearish +22 Bcf build surprise, and managed-money is net short at just the 8th percentile of its 5-year range, a crowded positioning that raises short-covering risk. Technicals are split by timeframe: 5m/15m lean bullish while 4h/1d/1w show a clear downtrend (EMA50<200, RSI ~37, negative MACD). Net effect: a modest fundamental/positioning tilt higher for the multi-week horizon, but no tradeable edge or trend confirmation for the next 1-10 days.

longTerm.tp2: $3.15 (winter curve premium building toward Nov/Dec contracts)
longTerm.riskReward: ~1:1.5 using TP2 (TP1 alone is closer to 1:0.85)
longTerm.sizing: 0.5R initial (reduced from standard 1R given low conviction); 1R approx 1% account risk
longTerm.rationale: Demand running well above 5-year normal in both weather windows and an extremely stretched managed-money short (8th percentile) support a mean-reversion long, and the fair-value curve rises through +21D as the calendar moves toward the winter build. This is offset by a growing storage surplus (+180 Bcf vs 5y) and bearish daily/weekly technicals, and by the total absence of seasonality data, so size and conviction are kept low.
longTerm.invalidation: A daily close below $2.60, or the storage surplus widening materially beyond +200 Bcf vs 5y, would negate the demand/positioning-driven long case.

shortTerm.rationale: Front price is trading almost exactly at fair value ($2.885 vs $2.923 fair, -$0.038 spread, FAIR status) so there is no valuation edge, and technicals conflict by timeframe: 5m/15m bullish (EMA50>200) versus 4h/1d/1w bearish (EMA50<200, RSI ~37-50). The 1-7D weather window is only modestly above normal (+4.3%, rangePos 0.6), not extreme enough to force a near-term directional call.
shortTerm.trigger: Wait for either a daily close above $2.95 with the 4h EMA50 crossing back above EMA200 (bullish reversal confirmation), or a breakdown below $2.75 confirming bearish continuation.
